I'm weighing the safest approach here. Removing `@Model` classes from the schema can cause data migration issues on existing installs, so keeping `CollectionEntity` in the schema while removing its UI would be the conservative choice. But since this is a development project and we're replacing collections entirely, I should remove `CollectionEntity` from the schema, add `SavedPlace` instead, and strip the collection property from `SavedSpotEntity`. The ticket is clear—I need to remove the deprecated Collections model and all its associated UI and logic. So I'm looking at removing the Collections UI files, deleting `CollectionEntity` from the schema, removing the collection relationship from `SavedSpotEntity`, and cleaning up all the routes and sheets that reference collections. I should check what's actually tied to this—the relationship field in `SavedSpotEntity`, the schema definition, the tab view that routes to collections screens, the route and sheet enum cases, and the `SavedCollectionsView` itself.

Now I'm tracing through the remaining references: `CollectionDetailView` and `NewCollectionSheet` both work with `CollectionEntity`, the `SavedSpotsService` likely has collection management methods, and the hamburger menu probably has a "Saved" button that navigates to the collections view. The real question is whether the ticket wants me to remove just the Collections UI or both `CollectionEntity` and `SavedSpotEntity` entirely. Looking at the ticket description, it says the new `SavedPlace` model replaces both the old "Saved Places" and "Collections" features, which suggests both systems are being deprecated. But `SavedSpotEntity` is also connected to the rating system, so I need to be careful about what actually gets removed versus what stays.

Re-reading the acceptance criteria, it specifically says to remove the "Collections" model and associated UI—that's `CollectionEntity`. The `SavedSpotEntity` is part of the broader "Saved Places" system, not the Collections system specifically. Looking at the current app structure, `SavedCollectionsView` has two tabs: one for Collections (showing `CollectionEntity`) and one for "All spots" (showing `SavedSpotEntity`). So the scope here is to remove the Collections functionality while the "All spots" tab gets replaced by the new `SavedPlace` model.

But I'm realizing there's a complication: `SavedSpotEntity` is still being used in the POI rating system through `SaveRatingMenu`, which calls `SavedSpotRating` and `SavedSpotsService`. This is separate from the new `SavedPlace` model—`SavedSpotEntity` stores ratings for POIs, while `SavedPlace` is for manually saved places with a status. I need to figure out whether the rating system should continue using `SavedSpotEntity` or if it should migrate to the new model.

Looking at the status values, they actually align perfectly: `SavedPlaceStatus` has `.wantToVisit`, `.good`, and `.dontGo`, which map directly to the `SavedSpotRating` cases. So `SavedPlace` could replace `SavedSpotEntity` for rated POIs, but removing `SavedSpotEntity` from the schema would break the existing POI sheet rating functionality.

For now, I'll take a pragmatic approach and focus on what the ticket explicitly asks for: removing `CollectionEntity` from the schema and deleting it.

My plan is to remove the collection UI files and routes, strip collection-related fields from `SavedSpotEntity`, update the main tab routing to use the new `SavedPlacesView`, add reverse geocoding to `LocationService`, and introduce `SavedPlace` to the schema while keeping `SavedSpotEntity` intact since the POI rating system still depends on it.

2	id: "TICKET-002"
3	title: "Saved places and Collections refactor"
4	status: "todo"
5	created_at: "2026-04-30T20:02:40.492Z"
6	updated_at: "2026-04-30T20:03:04.742Z"
7	---
8	
9	This plan outlines the implementation of a unified "Saved" system in your Unknown project, replacing separate "Saved Places" and "Collections" features. It focuses on automatic organization, a single-tap save mechanism, and a dynamic, faceted UI for browsing saved locations.
10	
11	## 1. Summary
12	This feature refactors the existing "Saved Places" and "Collections" into a single, cohesive "Saved" library. It introduces automatic organization of saved locations based on metadata such as country, city, and place type, along with their visit status. Users will benefit from frictionless saving via a single-tap action and a powerful, dynamic filter bar in the UI to group and browse their saved places.
13	
14	## 2. Files to create
15	*   `Citisen/Models/SavedPlace.swift`: Defines the SwiftData model for a saved location, including new metadata.
16	*   `Citisen/Models/SavedPlaceStatus.swift`: An enum to represent the status of a saved place (e.g., `wantToVisit`, `good`, `dontGo`).
17	*   `Citisen/Models/PlaceType.swift`: An enum to categorize saved places (e.g., `restaurant`, `museum`).
18	*   `Citisen/Services/LocationService.swift`: A service to perform reverse geocoding for location metadata.
19	*   `Citisen/ViewModels/SavedPlacesViewModel.swift`: An `@Observable` view model to manage saved places data, filtering, and grouping logic.
20	*   `Citisen/Views/SavedPlacesView/SavedPlacesView.swift`: The main SwiftUI view for the "Saved" library, displaying the filter bar and grouped list.
21	*   `Citisen/Views/SavedPlacesView/SavedPlaceRowView.swift`: A compact SwiftUI view for displaying an individual saved place in the list.
22	*   `Citisen/Views/SavedPlacesView/FilterBarView.swift`: A reusable SwiftUI component for the toggle-based filter bar.
23	*   `Citisen/Utilities/CountryCodeToEmoji.swift`: A helper extension to convert country codes to emoji flags.
24	
25	## 3. Files to modify
26	*   `Citisen/CitisenApp.swift`: Update the `ModelContainer` to include the new `SavedPlace` schema.
27	*   `Citisen/ContentView.swift` (or relevant main tab view): Integrate `SavedPlacesView` into the app's main navigation.
28	*   `Citisen/Info.plist`: Add `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription` for CoreLocation permissions.
29	*   *(Existing Place or SavedPlace model)*: If an existing `Place` or `SavedPlace` model exists, it should be either adapted to the new `SavedPlace` model or replaced entirely. For this plan, we assume a new `SavedPlace` model is created from scratch.
30	*   *(Existing Collections model)*: This model should be deprecated and removed, along with any associated UI and logic.
31	
32	## 4. Step-by-step implementation
33	
34	### Step 1: Define Data Models
35	1.  **Create `SavedPlaceStatus.swift`**: Define an enum for the different states a saved place can have.
36	    ```swift
37	    // Citisen/Models/SavedPlaceStatus.swift
38	    import Foundation
39	
40	    enum SavedPlaceStatus: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
41	        case wantToVisit = "Want to Visit"
42	        case good = "Good"
43	        case dontGo = "Don't Go"
44	
45	        var id: String { rawValue }
46	    }
47	    ```
48	2.  **Create `PlaceType.swift`**: Define an enum for categorizing places based on their "main travel mode association."
49	    ```swift
50	    // Citisen/Models/PlaceType.swift
51	    import Foundation
52	
53	    enum PlaceType: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
54	        case restaurant = "Restaurant"
55	        case museum = "Museum"
56	        case hotel = "Hotel"
57	        case park = "Park"
58	        case landmark = "Landmark"
59	        case cafe = "Cafe"
60	        case shopping = "Shopping"
61	        case other = "Other"
62	
63	        var id: String { rawValue }
64	    }
65	    ```
66	3.  **Create `SavedPlace.swift`**: Define the SwiftData model for saved places.
67	    ```swift
68	    // Citisen/Models/SavedPlace.swift
69	    import Foundation
70	    import SwiftData
71	    import CoreLocation // For CLLocationCoordinate2D
72	
73	    @Model
74	    class SavedPlace {
75	        // Indexing for efficient filtering and grouping
76	        #Index<SavedPlace>([\.countryCode], [\.city], [\.placeType], [\.status])
77	
78	        var id: UUID
79	        var name: String
80	        var latitude: Double
81	        var longitude: Double
82	        var countryCode: String? // e.g., "US", "IT"
83	        var city: String?
84	        var status: SavedPlaceStatus
85	        var placeType: PlaceType
86	        var timestamp: Date // When the place was saved
87	
88	        init(id: UUID = UUID(), name: String, latitude: Double, longitude: Double,
89	             countryCode: String? = nil, city: String? = nil,
90	             status: SavedPlaceStatus = .wantToVisit, placeType: PlaceType = .other,
91	             timestamp: Date = .now) {
92	            self.id = id
93	            self.name = name
94	            self.latitude = latitude
95	            self.longitude = longitude
96	            self.countryCode = countryCode
97	            self.city = city
98	            self.status = status
99	            self.placeType = placeType
100	            self.timestamp = timestamp
101	        }
102	
103	        var coordinate: CLLocationCoordinate2D {
104	            CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude)
105	        }
106	    }
107	    ```
108	
109	### Step 2: Implement Location Service
110	1.  **Create `LocationService.swift`**: Implement a service to perform reverse geocoding.
111	    ```swift
112	    // Citisen/Services/LocationService.swift
113	    import CoreLocation
114	    import Foundation
115	
116	    @MainActor
117	    class LocationService: NSObject, ObservableObject, CLLocationManagerDelegate {
118	        private let geocoder = CLGeocoder()
119	        private let locationManager = CLLocationManager()
120	
121	        override init() {
122	            super.init()
123	            locationManager.delegate = self
124	        }
125	
126	        func requestLocationPermissions() {
127	            locationManager.requestWhenInUseAuthorization()
128	        }
129	
130	        func reverseGeocode(location: CLLocation) async throws -> (countryCode: String?, city: String?) {
131	            let placemarks = try await geocoder.reverseGeocodeLocation(location)
132	            let placemark = placemarks.first
133	            return (placemark?.isoCountryCode, placemark?.locality)
134	        }
135	
136	        // CLLocationManagerDelegate methods (for permission handling)
137	        func locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization(_ manager: CLLocationManager) {
138	            // Handle authorization status changes if needed
139	            switch manager.authorizationStatus {
140	            case .authorizedWhenInUse, .authorizedAlways:
141	                print("Location access granted.")
142	            case .denied, .restricted:
143	                print("Location access denied or restricted.")
144	            case .notDetermined:
145	                print("Location authorization not determined.")
146	            @unknown default:
147	                print("Unknown location authorization status.")
148	            }
149	        }
150	    }
151	    ```

159	### Step 3: Create View Model
160	1.  **Create `SavedPlacesViewModel.swift`**: This view model will manage the data, filtering, and grouping logic.
161	    ```swift
162	    // Citisen/ViewModels/SavedPlacesViewModel.swift
163	    import Foundation
164	    import SwiftData
165	    import SwiftUI
166	    import CoreLocation
167	
168	    enum GroupingCriterion: String, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
169	        case country = "Country"
170	        case city = "City"
171	        case placeType = "Place Type"
172	        case status = "Saved Status"
173	
174	        var id: String { rawValue }
175	    }
176	
177	    struct GroupedSection: Identifiable {
178	        let id = UUID()
179	        let header: String
180	        let items: [SavedPlace]
181	    }
182	
183	    @MainActor
184	    @Observable
185	    class SavedPlacesViewModel {
186	        var modelContext: ModelContext
187	        var locationService: LocationService
188	
189	        @Query(sort: \SavedPlace.timestamp, order: .reverse) private var allSavedPlaces: [SavedPlace]
190	
191	        var selectedGroupingCriterion: GroupingCriterion = .country
192	        var searchText: String = ""
193	
194	        init(modelContext: ModelContext, locationService: LocationService) {
195	            self.modelContext = modelContext
196	            self.locationService = locationService
197	        }
198	
199	        var filteredAndGroupedPlaces: [GroupedSection] {
200	            let filteredPlaces = allSavedPlaces.filter { place in
201	                searchText.isEmpty || place.name.localizedStandardContains(searchText) ||
202	                (place.city?.localizedStandardContains(searchText) ?? false) ||
203	                (place.countryCode?.localizedStandardContains(searchText) ?? false)
204	            }
205	
206	            let groupedDictionary: [String: [SavedPlace]]
207	            switch selectedGroupingCriterion {
208	            case .country:
209	                groupedDictionary = Dictionary(grouping: filteredPlaces) { $0.countryCode ?? "Unknown Country" }
210	            case .city:
211	                groupedDictionary = Dictionary(grouping: filteredPlaces) { $0.city ?? "Unknown City" }
212	            case .placeType:
213	                groupedDictionary = Dictionary(grouping: filteredPlaces) { $0.placeType.rawValue }
214	            case .status:
215	                groupedDictionary = Dictionary(grouping: filteredPlaces) { $0.status.rawValue }
216	            }
217	
218	            // Sort groups by key (e.g., country name, city name) and items within groups by timestamp
219	            return groupedDictionary.map { (key, value) in
220	                GroupedSection(header: key, items: value.sorted(using: KeyPathComparator(\.timestamp, order: .reverse)))
221	            }
222	            .sorted { section1, section2 in
223	                // Custom sorting for country codes to put "Unknown Country" last, otherwise alphabetical
224	                if selectedGroupingCriterion == .country {
225	                    if section1.header == "Unknown Country" { return false }
226	                    if section2.header == "Unknown Country" { return true }
227	                }
228	                return section1.header < section2.header
229	            }
230	        }
231	
232	        func savePlace(name: String, latitude: Double, longitude: Double, placeType: PlaceType) async {
233	            let location = CLLocation(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude)
234	            var countryCode: String?
235	            var city: String?
236	
237	            do {
238	                (countryCode, city) = try await locationService.reverseGeocode(location: location)
239	            } catch {
240	                print("Error reverse geocoding: \(error.localizedDescription)")
241	            }
242	
243	            let newPlace = SavedPlace(name: name, latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude,
244	                                      countryCode: countryCode, city: city,
245	                                      status: .wantToVisit, placeType: placeType)
246	            modelContext.insert(newPlace)
247	            do {
248	                try modelContext.save()
249	            } catch {
250	                print("Error saving new place: \(error.localizedDescription)")
251	            }
252	        }
253	
254	        func updatePlaceStatus(place: SavedPlace, newStatus: SavedPlaceStatus) {
255	            place.status = newStatus
256	            do {
257	                try modelContext.save()
258	            } catch {
259	                print("Error updating place status: \(error.localizedDescription)")
260	            }
261	        }
262	
263	        func deletePlace(place: SavedPlace) {
264	            modelContext.delete(place)
265	            do {
266	                try modelContext.save()
267	            } catch {
268	                print("Error deleting place: \(error.localizedDescription)")
269	            }
270	        }
271	    }
272	    ```

276	1.  **Create `CountryCodeToEmoji.swift`**: Helper for country flags.
277	    ```swift
278	    // Citisen/Utilities/CountryCodeToEmoji.swift
279	    import Foundation
280	
281	    extension String {
282	        func flagEmoji() -> String {
283	            guard isCountryCode else { return "" }
284	            let base: UInt32 = 0x1F1E6
285	            var s = ""
286	            for v in unicodeScalars {
287	                s.unicodeScalars.append(UnicodeScalar(base + v.value - 0x41)!)
288	            }
289	            return String(s)
290	        }
291	
292	        var isCountryCode: Bool {
293	            self.count == 2 && self.range(of: "^[A-Z]{2}$", options: .regularExpression) != nil
294	        }
295	    }

559	## 6. Acceptance criteria
560	*   A new `SavedPlace` SwiftData model is created, including `id`, `name`, `latitude`, `longitude`, `countryCode`, `city`, `status`, `placeType`, and `timestamp`.
561	*   The `SavedPlace` model has appropriate indexes on `countryCode`, `city`, `placeType`, and `status` for efficient querying.
562	*   A "single-tap save" action (simulated by the "Add Place" button in `SavedPlacesView`) successfully creates a new `SavedPlace` instance with `status` set to `.wantToVisit`.
563	*   Upon saving, the `countryCode` and `city` are automatically populated via reverse geocoding using `LocationService`.
564	*   Users can change the `status` of a `SavedPlace` (e.g., to `.good` or `.dontGo`) via a context menu, and this change is persisted.
565	*   The `FilterBarView` is displayed at the top, allowing users to select grouping criteria (`Country`, `City`, `Place Type`, `Saved Status`).
566	*   The list of saved places dynamically re-groups based on the selected criterion in the filter bar.
567	*   When grouped by `Country`, section headers display the country name and its emoji flag (e.g., "🇮🇹 Italy").
568	*   Individual saved places are displayed using the `SavedPlaceRowView` with a compact design, showing the place name, city, country (with flag), and current status.
569	*   The list supports searching by place name, city, or country code.
570	*   The deprecated "Collections" model and any associated UI/logic are removed from the project.
571	*   Location permissions are requested when `SavedPlacesView` appears.
572	
573	## 7. Risks & notes
574	*   **CoreLocation Permissions:** The app will need `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription` in `Info.plist`. Users might deny location access, in which case `countryCode` and `city` will remain `nil` for newly saved places. The UI should gracefully handle missing location data.
575	*   **Geocoding Rate Limits/Reliability:** `CLGeocoder` has rate limits. Excessive rapid saving might lead to temporary geocoding failures. Consider adding a retry mechanism or displaying a message if geocoding fails. Network connectivity is also required for geocoding.
576	*   **Performance with Large Datasets:** While SwiftData's `@Query` and indexing help, complex dynamic grouping and filtering on very large datasets (thousands of places) might introduce UI lag. The current `filteredAndGroupedPlaces` computed property re-groups all items on every change. For extremely large datasets, consider more advanced caching or pagination strategies.
577	*   **Place Type Derivation:** The feature request mentions "main travel mode association of the place saved." In this plan, `placeType` is a direct property set during the save action. If this needs to be inferred automatically (e.g., from an external API or user behavior), that would require additional logic and potential API integration.
578	*   **SwiftData CloudKit Compatibility:** If CloudKit is enabled for SwiftData, ensure all `SavedPlace` properties have default values or are optional, and relationships are optional. Also, `@Attribute(.unique)` is not supported with CloudKit. This plan adheres to these rules.
579	*   **Error Handling:** The `savePlace` and `updatePlaceStatus` methods include basic `print` statements for errors. In a production app, these should be replaced with user-facing alerts or robust logging.
580	*   **UI/UX for "Single-Tap Save":** The plan includes an "Add Place" button for demonstration. The actual "single-tap save" would likely be integrated into a map view or detail view of a place, perhaps as a heart/bookmark icon that triggers `viewModel.savePlace` with the current place's data. This integration point is outside the scope of this specific plan but should be considered.
581	*   **Localization:** Country names derived from `Locale.current.localizedString(forRegionCode:)` will be localized, but other strings (like `PlaceType` raw values or `SavedPlaceStatus` raw values) will need to be localized via `Localizable.xcstrings` if the app supports multiple languages."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3dfc0c98-4980-4ee1-b7b7-218dd3fb4997","uuid":"42110872-44b6-42c6-8d02-e23aabaca9bd","timestamp":"2026-05-02T13:25:44.649Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/joaocaetano/Development/Ticket Manager/tickets/2_todo/TICKET-002.md","content":"---

This plan outlines the implementation of a unified "Saved" system in your Unknown project, replacing separate "Saved Places" and "Collections" features. It focuses on automatic organization, a single-tap save mechanism, and a dynamic, faceted UI for browsing saved locations.

## 1. Summary
This feature refactors the existing "Saved Places" and "Collections" into a single, cohesive "Saved" library. It introduces automatic organization of saved locations based on metadata such as country, city, and place type, along with their visit status. Users will benefit from frictionless saving via a single-tap action and a powerful, dynamic filter bar in the UI to group and browse their saved places.

## 2. Files to create
*   `Citisen/Models/SavedPlace.swift`: Defines the SwiftData model for a saved location, including new metadata.
*   `Citisen/Models/SavedPlaceStatus.swift`: An enum to represent the status of a saved place (e.g., `wantToVisit`, `good`, `dontGo`).
*   `Citisen/Models/PlaceType.swift`: An enum to categorize saved places (e.g., `restaurant`, `museum`).
*   `Citisen/Services/LocationService.swift`: A service to perform reverse geocoding for location metadata.
*   `Citisen/ViewModels/SavedPlacesViewModel.swift`: An `@Observable` view model to manage saved places data, filtering, and grouping logic.
*   `Citisen/Views/SavedPlacesView/SavedPlacesView.swift`: The main SwiftUI view for the "Saved" library, displaying the filter bar and grouped list.
*   `Citisen/Views/SavedPlacesView/SavedPlaceRowView.swift`: A compact SwiftUI view for displaying an individual saved place in the list.
*   `Citisen/Views/SavedPlacesView/FilterBarView.swift`: A reusable SwiftUI component for the toggle-based filter bar.
*   `Citisen/Utilities/CountryCodeToEmoji.swift`: A helper extension to convert country codes to emoji flags.

## 3. Files to modify
*   `Citisen/CitisenApp.swift`: Update the `ModelContainer` to include the new `SavedPlace` schema.
*   `Citisen/ContentView.swift` (or relevant main tab view): Integrate `SavedPlacesView` into the app's main navigation.
*   `Citisen/Info.plist`: Add `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription` for CoreLocation permissions.
*   *(Existing Place or SavedPlace model)*: If an existing `Place` or `SavedPlace` model exists, it should be either adapted to the new `SavedPlace` model or replaced entirely. For this plan, we assume a new `SavedPlace` model is created from scratch.
*   *(Existing Collections model)*: This model should be deprecated and removed, along with any associated UI and logic.

## 4. Step-by-step implementation

### Step 1: Define Data Models
1.  **Create `SavedPlaceStatus.swift`**: Define an enum for the different states a saved place can have.
    ```swift
    // Citisen/Models/SavedPlaceStatus.swift
    import Foundation

    enum SavedPlaceStatus: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
        case wantToVisit = "Want to Visit"
        case good = "Good"
        case dontGo = "Don't Go"

        var id: String { rawValue }
    }
    ```
2.  **Create `PlaceType.swift`**: Define an enum for categorizing places based on their "main travel mode association."
    ```swift
    // Citisen/Models/PlaceType.swift
    import Foundation

    enum PlaceType: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
        case restaurant = "Restaurant"
        case museum = "Museum"
        case hotel = "Hotel"
        case park = "Park"
        case landmark = "Landmark"
        case cafe = "Cafe"
        case shopping = "Shopping"
        case other = "Other"

        var id: String { rawValue }
    }
    ```
3.  **Create `SavedPlace.swift`**: Define the SwiftData model for saved places.
    ```swift
    // Citisen/Models/SavedPlace.swift
    import Foundation
    import SwiftData
    import CoreLocation // For CLLocationCoordinate2D

    @Model
    class SavedPlace {
        // Indexing for efficient filtering and grouping
        #Index<SavedPlace>([\.countryCode], [\.city], [\.placeType], [\.status])

        var id: UUID
        var name: String
        var latitude: Double
        var longitude: Double
        var countryCode: String? // e.g., "US", "IT"
        var city: String?
        var status: SavedPlaceStatus
        var placeType: PlaceType
        var timestamp: Date // When the place was saved

        init(id: UUID = UUID(), name: String, latitude: Double, longitude: Double,
             countryCode: String? = nil, city: String? = nil,
             status: SavedPlaceStatus = .wantToVisit, placeType: PlaceType = .other,
             timestamp: Date = .now) {
            self.id = id
            self.name = name
            self.latitude = latitude
            self.longitude = longitude
            self.countryCode = countryCode
            self.city = city
            self.status = status
            self.placeType = placeType
            self.timestamp = timestamp
        }

        var coordinate: CLLocationCoordinate2D {
            CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude)
        }
    }
    ```

### Step 2: Implement Location Service
1.  **Create `LocationService.swift`**: Implement a service to perform reverse geocoding.
    ```swift
    // Citisen/Services/LocationService.swift
    import CoreLocation
    import Foundation

    @MainActor
    class LocationService: NSObject, ObservableObject, CLLocationManagerDelegate {
        private let geocoder = CLGeocoder()
        private let locationManager = CLLocationManager()

        override init() {
            super.init()
            locationManager.delegate = self
        }

        func requestLocationPermissions() {
            locationManager.requestWhenInUseAuthorization()
        }

        func reverseGeocode(location: CLLocation) async throws -> (countryCode: String?, city: String?) {
            let placemarks = try await geocoder.reverseGeocodeLocation(location)
            let placemark = placemarks.first
            return (placemark?.isoCountryCode, placemark?.locality)
        }

        // CLLocationManagerDelegate methods (for permission handling)
        func locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization(_ manager: CLLocationManager) {
            // Handle authorization status changes if needed
            switch manager.authorizationStatus {
            case .authorizedWhenInUse, .authorizedAlways:
                print("Location access granted.")
            case .denied, .restricted:
                print("Location access denied or restricted.")
            case .notDetermined:
                print("Location authorization not determined.")
            @unknown default:
                print("Unknown location authorization status.")
            }
        }
    }
    ```
2.  **Update `Info.plist`**: Add the privacy description for location usage.

### Step 3: Create View Model
1.  **Create `SavedPlacesViewModel.swift`**: This view model will manage the data, filtering, and grouping logic.
    ```swift
    // Citisen/ViewModels/SavedPlacesViewModel.swift
    import Foundation
    import SwiftData
    import SwiftUI
    import CoreLocation

    enum GroupingCriterion: String, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
        case country = "Country"
        case city = "City"
        case placeType = "Place Type"
        case status = "Saved Status"

        var id: String { rawValue }
    }

    struct GroupedSection: Identifiable {
        let id = UUID()
        let header: String
        let items: [SavedPlace]
    }

    @MainActor
    @Observable
    class SavedPlacesViewModel {
        var modelContext: ModelContext
        var locationService: LocationService

        @Query(sort: \SavedPlace.timestamp, order: .reverse) private var allSavedPlaces: [SavedPlace]

        var selectedGroupingCriterion: GroupingCriterion = .country
        var searchText: String = ""

        init(modelContext: ModelContext, locationService: LocationService) {
            self.modelContext = modelContext
            self.locationService = locationService
        }

        var filteredAndGroupedPlaces: [GroupedSection] {
            let filteredPlaces = allSavedPlaces.filter { place in
                searchText.isEmpty || place.name.localizedStandardContains(searchText) ||
                (place.city?.localizedStandardContains(searchText) ?? false) ||
                (place.countryCode?.localizedStandardContains(searchText) ?? false)
            }

            let groupedDictionary: [String: [SavedPlace]]
            switch selectedGroupingCriterion {
            case .country:
                groupedDictionary = Dictionary(grouping: filteredPlaces) { $0.countryCode ?? "Unknown Country" }
            case .city:
                groupedDictionary = Dictionary(grouping: filteredPlaces) { $0.city ?? "Unknown City" }
            case .placeType:
                groupedDictionary = Dictionary(grouping: filteredPlaces) { $0.placeType.rawValue }
            case .status:
                groupedDictionary = Dictionary(grouping: filteredPlaces) { $0.status.rawValue }
            }

            // Sort groups by key (e.g., country name, city name) and items within groups by timestamp
            return groupedDictionary.map { (key, value) in
                GroupedSection(header: key, items: value.sorted(using: KeyPathComparator(\.timestamp, order: .reverse)))
            }
            .sorted { section1, section2 in
                // Custom sorting for country codes to put "Unknown Country" last, otherwise alphabetical
                if selectedGroupingCriterion == .country {
                    if section1.header == "Unknown Country" { return false }
                    if section2.header == "Unknown Country" { return true }
                }
                return section1.header < section2.header
            }
        }

        func savePlace(name: String, latitude: Double, longitude: Double, placeType: PlaceType) async {
            let location = CLLocation(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude)
            var countryCode: String?
            var city: String?

            do {
                (countryCode, city) = try await locationService.reverseGeocode(location: location)
            } catch {
                print("Error reverse geocoding: \(error.localizedDescription)")
            }

            let newPlace = SavedPlace(name: name, latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude,
                                      countryCode: countryCode, city: city,
                                      status: .wantToVisit, placeType: placeType)
            modelContext.insert(newPlace)
            do {
                try modelContext.save()
            } catch {
                print("Error saving new place: \(error.localizedDescription)")
            }
        }

        func updatePlaceStatus(place: SavedPlace, newStatus: SavedPlaceStatus) {
            place.status = newStatus
            do {
                try modelContext.save()
            } catch {
                print("Error updating place status: \(error.localizedDescription)")
            }
        }

        func deletePlace(place: SavedPlace) {
            modelContext.delete(place)
            do {
                try modelContext.save()
            } catch {
                print("Error deleting place: \(error.localizedDescription)")
            }
        }
    }
    ```

### Step 4: Create UI Components

1.  **Create `CountryCodeToEmoji.swift`**: Helper for country flags.
    ```swift
    // Citisen/Utilities/CountryCodeToEmoji.swift
    import Foundation

    extension String {
        func flagEmoji() -> String {
            guard isCountryCode else { return "" }
            let base: UInt32 = 0x1F1E6
            var s = ""
            for v in unicodeScalars {
                s.unicodeScalars.append(UnicodeScalar(base + v.value - 0x41)!)
            }
            return String(s)
        }

        var isCountryCode: Bool {
            self.count == 2 && self.range(of: "^[A-Z]{2}$", options: .regularExpression) != nil
        }
    }
    ```

    // Citisen/Views/SavedPlacesView/FilterBarView.swift

                .padding(.horizontal)

3.  **Create `SavedPlaceRowView.swift`**: Compact row design.
    ```swift
    // Citisen/Views/SavedPlacesView/SavedPlaceRowView.swift
    import SwiftUI

    struct SavedPlaceRowView: View {
        let place: SavedPlace

        var body: some View {
            HStack {
                VStack(alignment: .leading) {
                    Text(place.name)
                        .font(.headline)
                        .lineLimit(1)

                    HStack(spacing: 4) {
                        if let city = place.city {
                            Text(city)
                        }
                        if let countryCode = place.countryCode {
                            Text(countryCode.flagEmoji())
                            Text(Locale.current.localizedString(forRegionCode: countryCode) ?? countryCode)
                        }
                    }
                    .font(.subheadline)
                    .foregroundStyle(.secondary)
                    .lineLimit(1)
                }
                Spacer()
                Text(place.status.rawValue)
                    .font(.caption)
                    .padding(.horizontal, 6)
                    .padding(.vertical, 4)
                    .background(statusColor(for: place.status))
                    .clipShape(.rect(cornerRadius: 5))
                    .foregroundStyle(.white)
            }
            .padding(.vertical, 4)
        }

        private func statusColor(for status: SavedPlaceStatus) -> Color {
            switch status {
            case .wantToVisit: .blue
            case .good: .green
            case .dontGo: .red
            }
        }
    }
    ```
4.  **Create `SavedPlacesView.swift`**: The main view.
    ```swift
    // Citisen/Views/SavedPlacesView/SavedPlacesView.swift
    import SwiftUI
    import SwiftData
    import CoreLocation // For simulating a save action

    struct SavedPlacesView: View {
        @Environment(\.modelContext) private var modelContext
        @State private var viewModel: SavedPlacesViewModel
        @State private var showingAddPlaceSheet = false

        init(locationService: LocationService) {
            _viewModel = State(initialValue: SavedPlacesViewModel(modelContext: .init(ModelContainer.forPreview(SavedPlace.self)), locationService: locationService))
        }

        var body: some View {
            NavigationStack {
                VStack(spacing: 0) {
                    FilterBarView(viewModel: viewModel)
                        .padding(.vertical, 8)

                    Divider()

                    List {
                        if viewModel.filteredAndGroupedPlaces.isEmpty {
                            ContentUnavailableView.search(text: viewModel.searchText)
                        } else {
                            ForEach(viewModel.filteredAndGroupedPlaces) { section in
                                Section {
                                    ForEach(section.items) { place in
                                        SavedPlaceRowView(place: place)
                                            .swipeActions(edge: .trailing) {
                                                Button(role: .destructive) {
                                                    viewModel.deletePlace(place: place)
                                                } label: {
                                                    Label("Delete", systemImage: "trash")
                                                }
                                            }
                                            .contextMenu {
                                                ForEach(SavedPlaceStatus.allCases) { status in
                                                    Button {
                                                        viewModel.updatePlaceStatus(place: place, newStatus: status)
                                                    } label: {
                                                        Label(status.rawValue, systemImage: place.status == status ? "checkmark.circle.fill" : "circle")
                                                    }
                                                }
                                            }
                                    }
                                } header: {
                                    HStack {
                                        if viewModel.selectedGroupingCriterion == .country, let countryCode = section.items.first?.countryCode {
                                            Text(countryCode.flagEmoji())
                                        }
                                        Text(section.header)
                                    }
                                    .font(.headline)
                                    .textCase(nil) // Prevent uppercase for section headers
                                }
                            }
                        }
                    }
                    .listStyle(.plain)
                    .searchable(text: $viewModel.searchText, placement: .navigationBarDrawer(displayMode: .always))
                }
                .navigationTitle("Saved Places")
                .toolbar {
                    ToolbarItem(placement: .topBarTrailing) {
                        Button("Add Place", systemImage: "plus.circle.fill") {
                            showingAddPlaceSheet = true
                        }
                    }
                }
                .sheet(isPresented: $showingAddPlaceSheet) {
                    // Placeholder for a simple "Add Place" sheet
                    AddPlaceSheet(viewModel: viewModel, isShowingSheet: $showingAddPlaceSheet)
                }
            }
            .onAppear {
                viewModel.modelContext = modelContext // Ensure ViewModel uses the correct context
                viewModel.locationService.requestLocationPermissions()
            }
        }
    }

    // Simple sheet for adding a new place (for demonstration)
    struct AddPlaceSheet: View {
        @Bindable var viewModel: SavedPlacesViewModel
        @Binding var isShowingSheet: Bool

        @State private var placeName: String = ""
        @State private var latitude: String = ""
        @State private var longitude: String = ""
        @State private var selectedPlaceType: PlaceType = .other

        var body: some View {
            NavigationStack {
                Form {
                    TextField("Place Name", text: $placeName)
                    TextField("Latitude", text: $latitude)
                        .keyboardType(.decimalPad)
                    TextField("Longitude", text: $longitude)
                        .keyboardType(.decimalPad)
                    Picker("Place Type", selection: $selectedPlaceType) {
                        ForEach(PlaceType.allCases) { type in
                            Text(type.rawValue).tag(type)
                        }
                    }
                }
                .navigationTitle("New Saved Place")
                .navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(.inline)
                .toolbar {
                    ToolbarItem(placement: .topBarLeading) {
                        Button("Cancel") {
                            isShowingSheet = false
                        }
                    }
                    ToolbarItem(placement: .topBarTrailing) {
                        Button("Save") {
                            if let lat = Double(latitude), let lon = Double(longitude), !placeName.isEmpty {
                                Task {
                                    await viewModel.savePlace(name: placeName, latitude: lat, longitude: lon, placeType: selectedPlaceType)
                                    isShowingSheet = false
                                }
                            }
                        }
                        .disabled(placeName.isEmpty || latitude.isEmpty || longitude.isEmpty)
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    ```

## 6. Acceptance criteria
*   A new `SavedPlace` SwiftData model is created, including `id`, `name`, `latitude`, `longitude`, `countryCode`, `city`, `status`, `placeType`, and `timestamp`.
*   The `SavedPlace` model has appropriate indexes on `countryCode`, `city`, `placeType`, and `status` for efficient querying.
*   A "single-tap save" action (simulated by the "Add Place" button in `SavedPlacesView`) successfully creates a new `SavedPlace` instance with `status` set to `.wantToVisit`.
*   Upon saving, the `countryCode` and `city` are automatically populated via reverse geocoding using `LocationService`.
*   Users can change the `status` of a `SavedPlace` (e.g., to `.good` or `.dontGo`) via a context menu, and this change is persisted.
*   The `FilterBarView` is displayed at the top, allowing users to select grouping criteria (`Country`, `City`, `Place Type`, `Saved Status`).
*   The list of saved places dynamically re-groups based on the selected criterion in the filter bar.
*   When grouped by `Country`, section headers display the country name and its emoji flag (e.g., "🇮🇹 Italy").
*   Individual saved places are displayed using the `SavedPlaceRowView` with a compact design, showing the place name, city, country (with flag), and current status.
*   The list supports searching by place name, city, or country code.
*   The deprecated "Collections" model and any associated UI/logic are removed from the project.
*   Location permissions are requested when `SavedPlacesView` appears.

## 7. Risks & notes
*   **CoreLocation Permissions:** The app will need `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription` in `Info.plist`. Users might deny location access, in which case `countryCode` and `city` will remain `nil` for newly saved places. The UI should gracefully handle missing location data.
*   **Geocoding Rate Limits/Reliability:** `CLGeocoder` has rate limits. Excessive rapid saving might lead to temporary geocoding failures. Consider adding a retry mechanism or displaying a message if geocoding fails. Network connectivity is also required for geocoding.
*   **Performance with Large Datasets:** While SwiftData's `@Query` and indexing help, complex dynamic grouping and filtering on very large datasets (thousands of places) might introduce UI lag. The current `filteredAndGroupedPlaces` computed property re-groups all items on every change. For extremely large datasets, consider more advanced caching or pagination strategies.
*   **Place Type Derivation:** The feature request mentions "main travel mode association of the place saved." In this plan, `placeType` is a direct property set during the save action. If this needs to be inferred automatically (e.g., from an external API or user behavior), that would require additional logic and potential API integration.
*   **SwiftData CloudKit Compatibility:** If CloudKit is enabled for SwiftData, ensure all `SavedPlace` properties have default values or are optional, and relationships are optional. Also, `@Attribute(.unique)` is not supported with CloudKit. This plan adheres to these rules.
*   **Error Handling:** The `savePlace` and `updatePlaceStatus` methods include basic `print` statements for errors. In a production app, these should be replaced with user-facing alerts or robust logging.
*   **UI/UX for "Single-Tap Save":** The plan includes an "Add Place" button for demonstration. The actual "single-tap save" would likely be integrated into a map view or detail view of a place, perhaps as a heart/bookmark icon that triggers `viewModel.savePlace` with the current place's data. This integration point is outside the scope of this specific plan but should be considered.
*   **Localization:** Country names derived from `Locale.current.localizedString(forRegionCode:)` will be localized, but other strings (like `PlaceType` raw values or `SavedPlaceStatus` raw values) will need to be localized via `Localizable.xcstrings` if the app supports multiple languages.","numLines":581,"startLine":1,"totalLines":581}}}

     6	enum GroupingCriterion: String, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
     7	    case country = "Country"
     8	    case city = "City"
     9	    case placeType = "Place Type"
    10	    case status = "Saved Status"
    11	
    12	    var id: String { rawValue }

    15	struct GroupedSection: Identifiable {
    16	    let header: String
    17	    let items: [SavedPlace]
    18	    var id: String { header }
    19	}
    20	
    21	@MainActor
    22	@Observable
    23	final class SavedPlacesViewModel {
    24	    var selectedGroupingCriterion: GroupingCriterion = .country
    25	    var searchText: String = ""
    26	
    27	    private var modelContext: ModelContext
    28	
    29	    init(modelContext: ModelContext) {
    30	        self.modelContext = modelContext
    31	    }
    32	
    33	    func groupedSections(from places: [SavedPlace]) -> [GroupedSection] {
    34	        let filtered = places.filter { place in
    35	            searchText.isEmpty
    36	                || place.name.localizedStandardContains(searchText)
    37	                || (place.city?.localizedStandardContains(searchText) ?? false)
    38	                || (place.countryCode?.localizedStandardContains(searchText) ?? false)
    39	        }
    40	
    41	        let grouped: [String: [SavedPlace]]
    42	        switch selectedGroupingCriterion {
    43	        case .country:
    44	            grouped = Dictionary(grouping: filtered) { $0.countryCode ?? "Unknown Country" }
    45	        case .city:
    46	            grouped = Dictionary(grouping: filtered) { $0.city ?? "Unknown City" }
    47	        case .placeType:
    48	            grouped = Dictionary(grouping: filtered) { $0.placeType.rawValue }
    49	        case .status:
    50	            grouped = Dictionary(grouping: filtered) { $0.status.rawValue }
    51	        }
    52	
    53	        return grouped
    54	            .map { key, value in
    55	                GroupedSection(
    56	                    header: key,
    57	                    items: value.sorted(using: KeyPathComparator(\.timestamp, order: .reverse))
    58	                )
    59	            }
    60	            .sorted { lhs, rhs in
    61	                if selectedGroupingCriterion == .country {
    62	                    if lhs.header == "Unknown Country" { return false }
    63	                    if rhs.header == "Unknown Country" { return true }
    64	                }
    65	                return lhs.header < rhs.header
    66	            }
    67	    }
    68	
    69	    func savePlace(
    70	        name: String,
    71	        latitude: Double,
    72	        longitude: Double,
    73	        placeType: PlaceType,
    74	        locationService: LocationService
    75	    ) async {
    76	        let location = CLLocation(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude)
    77	        var countryCode: String?
    78	        var city: String?
    79	
    80	        do {
    81	            (countryCode, city) = try await locationService.reverseGeocode(location: location)
    82	        } catch {
    83	            AppLog.location.error("Reverse geocoding failed: \(error.localizedDescription, privacy: .public)")
    84	        }
    85	
    86	        let place = SavedPlace(
    87	            name: name,
    88	            latitude: latitude,
    89	            longitude: longitude,
    90	            countryCode: countryCode,
    91	            city: city,
    92	            status: .wantToVisit,
    93	            placeType: placeType
    94	        )
    95	        modelContext.insert(place)
    96	        try? modelContext.save()
    97	    }

    99	    func updateStatus(of place: SavedPlace, to status: SavedPlaceStatus) {
   100	        place.status = status
   101	        try? modelContext.save()
   102	    }
   103	
   104	    func delete(_ place: SavedPlace) {
   105	        modelContext.delete(place)
   106	        try? modelContext.save()
   107	    }
   108	}","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3dfc0c98-4980-4ee1-b7b7-218dd3fb4997","uuid":"cf0d9b66-e0f8-4db7-93c6-40959bb56d2f","timestamp":"2026-05-02T13:27:19.234Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"     1	import CoreLocation

   123	struct AddPlaceSheet: View {
   124	    @Bindable var viewModel: SavedPlacesViewModel
   125	    let locationService: LocationService
   126	    @Binding var isPresented: Bool
   127	
   128	    @State private var placeName = ""
   129	    @State private var latitude = ""
   130	    @State private var longitude = ""
   131	    @State private var selectedPlaceType: PlaceType = .other
   132	    @State private var isSaving = false
   133	
   134	    var body: some View {
   135	        NavigationStack {
   136	            Form {
   137	                TextField("Place Name", text: $placeName)
   138	                TextField("Latitude", text: $latitude)
   139	                    .keyboardType(.decimalPad)
   140	                TextField("Longitude", text: $longitude)
   141	                    .keyboardType(.decimalPad)
   142	                Picker("Place Type", selection: $selectedPlaceType) {
   143	                    ForEach(PlaceType.allCases) { type in
   144	                        Text(type.rawValue).tag(type)
   145	                    }
   146	                }
   147	            }
   148	            .navigationTitle("New Saved Place")
   149	            .navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(.inline)
   150	            .toolbar {
   151	                ToolbarItem(placement: .cancellationAction) {
   152	                    Button("Cancel") { isPresented = false }
   153	                        .tint(BrandColor.sand)
   154	                }
   155	                ToolbarItem(placement: .confirmationAction) {
   156	                    Button("Save") {
   157	                        guard let lat = Double(latitude), let lon = Double(longitude) else { return }
   158	                        isSaving = true
   159	                        Task {
   160	                            await viewModel.savePlace(
   161	                                name: placeName,
   162	                                latitude: lat,
   163	                                longitude: lon,
   164	                                placeType: selectedPlaceType,
   165	                                locationService: locationService
   166	                            )
   167	                            isPresented = false
   168	                        }
   169	                    }
   170	                    .disabled(placeName.isEmpty || latitude.isEmpty || longitude.isEmpty || isSaving)
   171	                    .tint(BrandColor.sand)
   172	                }
   173	            }
   174	        }
   175	    }
   176	}

import Observation
import os

@Observable
final class LocationService: NSObject, CLLocationManagerDelegate {
    var authorizationStatus: CLAuthorizationStatus
    var currentLocation: CLLocationCoordinate2D?
    var lastError: String?

    private let manager: CLLocationManager

    override init() {
        self.manager = CLLocationManager()
        self.authorizationStatus = manager.authorizationStatus
        super.init()
        manager.delegate = self
        manager.desiredAccuracy = kCLLocationAccuracyHundredMeters
    }

    func requestWhenInUse() {
        manager.requestWhenInUseAuthorization()
    }

    func startUpdating() {
        manager.startUpdatingLocation()
    }

    func stopUpdating() {
        manager.stopUpdatingLocation()
    }

    func locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization(_ manager: CLLocationManager) {
        authorizationStatus = manager.authorizationStatus
        AppLog.location.info("Authorization changed: \(self.authorizationStatus.rawValue)")
        if authorizationStatus == .authorizedWhenInUse || authorizationStatus == .authorizedAlways {
            manager.startUpdatingLocation()
        }
    }

    func locationManager(_ manager: CLLocationManager, didUpdateLocations locations: [CLLocation]) {
        guard let last = locations.last else { return }
        currentLocation = last.coordinate
    }

        lastError = error.localizedDescription

import Observation
import os

@Observable
final class LocationService: NSObject, CLLocationManagerDelegate {
    var authorizationStatus: CLAuthorizationStatus
    var currentLocation: CLLocationCoordinate2D?
    var lastError: String?

    private let manager: CLLocationManager

    override init() {
        self.manager = CLLocationManager()
        self.authorizationStatus = manager.authorizationStatus
        super.init()
        manager.delegate = self
        manager.desiredAccuracy = kCLLocationAccuracyHundredMeters
    }

    func requestWhenInUse() {
        manager.requestWhenInUseAuthorization()
    }

    func startUpdating() {
        manager.startUpdatingLocation()
    }

    func stopUpdating() {
        manager.stopUpdatingLocation()
    }

    func locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization(_ manager: CLLocationManager) {
        authorizationStatus = manager.authorizationStatus
        AppLog.location.info("Authorization changed: \(self.authorizationStatus.rawValue)")
        if authorizationStatus == .authorizedWhenInUse || authorizationStatus == .authorizedAlways {
            manager.startUpdatingLocation()
        }
    }

    func locationManager(_ manager: CLLocationManager, didUpdateLocations locations: [CLLocation]) {
        guard let last = locations.last else { return }
        currentLocation = last.coordinate
    }

    func locationManager(_ manager: CLLocationManager, didFailWithError error: Error) {
        lastError = error.localizedDescription
        AppLog.location.error("Location error: \(error.localizedDescription, privacy: .public)")
    }
}

struct MainTabHostView: View {
    @Environment(AppRouter.self)
    private var router
    @Environment(UserPreferencesService.self)
    private var prefs
    @Environment(CityService.self)
    private var city
    @Environment(MockPlacesService.self)
    private var places

    var body: some View {
        @Bindable var router = router

        NavigationStack(path: $router.path) {
            ZStack {
                MapScreen()
                    .ignoresSafeArea()

                if let toast = router.nearMeToast {
                    toastView(toast)
                        .transition(.move(edge: .top).combined(with: .opacity))
                }

                HamburgerMenuView()
                    .zIndex(100)
            }
            .navigationDestination(for: AppRoute.self, destination: destination(for:))
        }
        .sheet(item: $router.presentedSheet) { sheet in
            sheetContent(sheet)
        }
    }

    @ViewBuilder
    private func destination(for route: AppRoute) -> some View {
        switch route {
        case .profile:
            ProfileView()
        case .saved:
            SavedCollectionsView()
        case .collectionDetail(let id):
            CollectionDetailView(collectionId: id)
        case .about:
            WebContentView(
                title: "About Citisen",
                url: URL(string: AppConfig.aboutURLString)
            )
        case .privacy:
            WebContentView(
                title: "Privacy Policy",
                url: URL(string: AppConfig.privacyURLString)
            )
        }
    }

    @ViewBuilder
    private func sheetContent(_ sheet: AppSheet) -> some View {
        switch sheet {
        case .search:
            SearchView()
        case .citySwitcher:
            CitySwitcherSheet()
                .presentationDetents([.height(360), .medium])
                .presentationDragIndicator(.visible)
        case .modePicker(let slotIndex):
            modePickerSheet(slotIndex: slotIndex)
        case .newCollection:
            NewCollectionSheet()
                .presentationDetents([.height(420)])
                .presentationDragIndicator(.visible)
        case .poi(let placeId):
            poiSheet(for: placeId)
        }
    }

    @ViewBuilder
    private func modePickerSheet(slotIndex: Int) -> some View {
        @Bindable var prefs = prefs
        ModePickerSheet(
            currentMode: prefs.activeModes[safe: slotIndex] ?? .food,
            excluded: Set(prefs.activeModes).subtracting([prefs.activeModes[safe: slotIndex] ?? .food])
        ) { picked in
            prefs.replaceSlot(slotIndex, with: picked)
            router.dismissSheet()
        } onCancel: {
            router.dismissSheet()
        }
        .presentationDetents([.medium, .large])
        .presentationDragIndicator(.visible)
    }

    @ViewBuilder
    private func poiSheet(for placeId: UUID) -> some View {
        @Bindable var router = router
        if let place = places.place(id: placeId) {
            POISheetView(place: place)
                .presentationDetents(
                    [.height(340), .height(600), .large],
                    selection: $router.poiDetent
                )
                .presentationBackgroundInteraction(.enabled(upThrough: .height(600)))
                .presentationDragIndicator(.visible)
                .presentationBackground(AppColor.surfaceElevated)
        } else {
            VStack {
                Text("Place not found").font(.headline17)
            }
            .padding()
        }
    }

    private func toastView(_ text: String) -> some View {
        VStack {
            Text(text)
                .font(.callout16)
                .foregroundStyle(.white)
                .padding(.horizontal, Spacing.md)
                .padding(.vertical, Spacing.sm)
                .background(.black.opacity(0.8))
                .clipShape(Capsule())
                .padding(.top, 100)
            Spacer()
        }
    }
}

        indices.contains(index) ? self[index] : nil
    }
}

import SwiftData

@MainActor
final class SavedSpotsService {
    private let context: ModelContext

    init(context: ModelContext) {
        self.context = context
    }

    func savedRating(for placeId: UUID) -> SavedSpotRating? {
        spot(for: placeId)?.rating
    }

    func spot(for placeId: UUID) -> SavedSpotEntity? {
        var descriptor = FetchDescriptor<SavedSpotEntity>(
            predicate: #Predicate { $0.placeId == placeId }
        )
        descriptor.fetchLimit = 1
        return (try? context.fetch(descriptor))?.first
    }

    func setRating(_ rating: SavedSpotRating, for place: Place) {
        if let existing = spot(for: place.id) {
            if existing.rating == rating {
                context.delete(existing)
            } else {
                existing.ratingRaw = rating.rawValue
            }
        } else {
            let entity = SavedSpotEntity(
                placeId: place.id,
                placeName: place.name,
                placeCategory: place.category,
                mode: place.mode,
                rating: rating,
                cityId: place.cityId
            )
            context.insert(entity)
        }
        try? context.save()
    }

    func unsave(placeId: UUID) {
        guard let existing = spot(for: placeId) else { return }
        context.delete(existing)
        try? context.save()
    }

    func allSpots() -> [SavedSpotEntity] {
        let descriptor = FetchDescriptor<SavedSpotEntity>(
            sortBy: [SortDescriptor(\.savedAt, order: .reverse)]
        )
        return (try? context.fetch(descriptor)) ?? []
    }
}

import SwiftData

@MainActor
final class SavedSpotsService {
    private let context: ModelContext

    init(context: ModelContext) {
        self.context = context
    }

    // MARK: - Spots

    func savedRating(for placeId: UUID) -> SavedSpotRating? {
        spot(for: placeId)?.rating
    }

    func spot(for placeId: UUID) -> SavedSpotEntity? {
        var descriptor = FetchDescriptor<SavedSpotEntity>(
            predicate: #Predicate { $0.placeId == placeId }
        )
        descriptor.fetchLimit = 1
        return (try? context.fetch(descriptor))?.first
    }

    func setRating(_ rating: SavedSpotRating, for place: Place) {
        if let existing = spot(for: place.id) {
            if existing.rating == rating {
                context.delete(existing)
            } else {
                existing.ratingRaw = rating.rawValue
            }
        } else {
            let entity = SavedSpotEntity(
                placeId: place.id,
                placeName: place.name,
                placeCategory: place.category,
                mode: place.mode,
                rating: rating,
                cityId: place.cityId
            )
            context.insert(entity)
        }
        try? context.save()
    }

    func unsave(placeId: UUID) {
        guard let existing = spot(for: placeId) else { return }
        context.delete(existing)
        try? context.save()
    }

    func allSpots() -> [SavedSpotEntity] {
        let descriptor = FetchDescriptor<SavedSpotEntity>(
            sortBy: [SortDescriptor(\.savedAt, order: .reverse)]
        )
        return (try? context.fetch(descriptor)) ?? []
    }

    // MARK: - Collections

    func allCollections() -> [CollectionEntity] {
        let descriptor = FetchDescriptor<CollectionEntity>(
            sortBy: [SortDescriptor(\.createdAt, order: .reverse)]
        )
        return (try? context.fetch(descriptor)) ?? []
    }

    @discardableResult
    func createCollection(
        name: String,
        city: City,
        colorHexes: [String] = ["C8975A", "D4695E", "4A8C6F"],
        iconSymbol: String = "bookmark.fill"
    ) -> CollectionEntity {
        let entity = CollectionEntity(
            name: name,
            cityId: city.id,
            cityName: city.name,
            countryName: city.country,
            colorHexes: colorHexes,
            iconSymbol: iconSymbol
        )
        context.insert(entity)
        try? context.save()
        return entity
    }

    func delete(_ collection: CollectionEntity) {
        context.delete(collection)
        try? context.save()
    }

    func rename(_ collection: CollectionEntity, to name: String) {
        collection.name = name
        try? context.save()
    }

    func addSpot(_ spot: SavedSpotEntity, to collection: CollectionEntity) {
        spot.collection = collection
        try? context.save()
    }

    func removeSpot(_ spot: SavedSpotEntity) {
        context.delete(spot)
        try? context.save()
    }
}

import SwiftData

@MainActor
final class SavedSpotsService {
    private let context: ModelContext

    init(context: ModelContext) {
        self.context = context
    }

    // MARK: - Spots

    func savedRating(for placeId: UUID) -> SavedSpotRating? {
        spot(for: placeId)?.rating
    }

    func spot(for placeId: UUID) -> SavedSpotEntity? {
        var descriptor = FetchDescriptor<SavedSpotEntity>(
            predicate: #Predicate { $0.placeId == placeId }
        )
        descriptor.fetchLimit = 1
        return (try? context.fetch(descriptor))?.first
    }

    func setRating(_ rating: SavedSpotRating, for place: Place) {
        if let existing = spot(for: place.id) {
            if existing.rating == rating {
                context.delete(existing)
            } else {
                existing.ratingRaw = rating.rawValue
            }
        } else {
            let entity = SavedSpotEntity(
                placeId: place.id,
                placeName: place.name,
                placeCategory: place.category,
                mode: place.mode,
                rating: rating,
                cityId: place.cityId
            )
            context.insert(entity)
        }
        try? context.save()
    }

    func unsave(placeId: UUID) {
        guard let existing = spot(for: placeId) else { return }
        context.delete(existing)
        try? context.save()
    }

    func allSpots() -> [SavedSpotEntity] {
        let descriptor = FetchDescriptor<SavedSpotEntity>(
            sortBy: [SortDescriptor(\.savedAt, order: .reverse)]
        )
        return (try? context.fetch(descriptor)) ?? []
    }

    // MARK: - Collections

    func allCollections() -> [CollectionEntity] {
        let descriptor = FetchDescriptor<CollectionEntity>(
            sortBy: [SortDescriptor(\.createdAt, order: .reverse)]
        )
        return (try? context.fetch(descriptor)) ?? []
    }

    @discardableResult
    func createCollection(
        name: String,
        city: City,
        colorHexes: [String] = ["C8975A", "D4695E", "4A8C6F"],
        iconSymbol: String = "bookmark.fill"
    ) -> CollectionEntity {
        let entity = CollectionEntity(
            name: name,
            cityId: city.id,
            cityName: city.name,
            countryName: city.country,
            colorHexes: colorHexes,
            iconSymbol: iconSymbol
        )
        context.insert(entity)
        try? context.save()
        return entity
    }

    func delete(_ collection: CollectionEntity) {
        context.delete(collection)
        try? context.save()
    }

    func rename(_ collection: CollectionEntity, to name: String) {
        collection.name = name
        try? context.save()
    }

    func addSpot(_ spot: SavedSpotEntity, to collection: CollectionEntity) {
        spot.collection = collection
        try? context.save()
    }

    func removeSpot(_ spot: SavedSpotEntity) {
        context.delete(spot)
        try? context.save()
    }
}

import os
import SwiftData

enum AppModelSchema {
    static let models: [any PersistentModel.Type] = [
        SavedSpotEntity.self,
        CollectionEntity.self
    ]

    @MainActor
    static func makeContainer() -> ModelContainer {
        do {
            let schema = Schema(models)
            let config = ModelConfiguration(schema: schema, isStoredInMemoryOnly: false)
            let container = try ModelContainer(for: schema, configurations: [config])
            seedIfNeeded(container.mainContext)
            return container

struct HamburgerMenuView: View {
    @Environment(AppRouter.self)
    private var router
    @Environment(UserPreferencesService.self)
    private var prefs
    @Environment(CityService.self)
    private var city
    @Environment(\.openURL)
    private var openURL

    @State private var showAbout = false
    @State private var showPrivacy = false
    @State private var showFeedbackAlert = false

    var body: some View {
        ZStack(alignment: .trailing) {
            if router.isHamburgerOpen {
                Color.black.opacity(0.4)
                    .ignoresSafeArea()
                    .contentShape(Rectangle())
                    .onTapGesture { router.closeHamburger() }
                    .transition(.opacity)

                drawer
                    .transition(.move(edge: .trailing))
            }
        }
        .animation(.spring(response: 0.35, dampingFraction: 0.85), value: router.isHamburgerOpen)
        .sheet(isPresented: $showAbout) {
            if let url = URL(string: AppConfig.aboutURLString) {
                SafariView(url: url)
            }
        }
        .sheet(isPresented: $showPrivacy) {
            if let url = URL(string: AppConfig.privacyURLString) {
                SafariView(url: url)
            }
        }
        .alert("Feedback", isPresented: $showFeedbackAlert) {
            Button("Send email") {
                if let url = URL(string: "mailto:\(AppConfig.feedbackEmail)?subject=Citisen%20feedback") {
                    openURL(url)
                }
            }
            Button("Cancel", role: .cancel) {}
        } message: {
            Text("Reach us at \(AppConfig.feedbackEmail).")
        }
    }

    private var drawer: some View {
        VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 0) {
            header
                .padding(.top, 64)
                .padding(.horizontal, Spacing.lg)
                .padding(.bottom, Spacing.lg)

            Divider().background(AppColor.dividerSoft).padding(.horizontal, Spacing.lg)

            ScrollView {
                VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 0) {
                    row("magnifyingglass", "Search") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.present(.search)
                    }
                    row("bookmark", "Saved") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.push(.saved)
                    }
                    row("map", "Collections") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.push(.saved)
                    }
                    row("mappin.circle", "Change city") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.present(.citySwitcher)
                    }

                    Divider().background(AppColor.dividerSoft).padding(.vertical, 8).padding(.horizontal, Spacing.lg)

                    row("gearshape", "Preferences") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.push(.profile)
                    }
                    row("person.crop.circle", "Profile") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.push(.profile)
                    }
                    row("info.circle", "About Citisen") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        showAbout = true
                    }
                    row("lock.shield", "Privacy") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        showPrivacy = true
                    }
                    row("envelope", "Send feedback") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        showFeedbackAlert = true
                    }

                    Divider().background(AppColor.dividerSoft).padding(.vertical, 8).padding(.horizontal, Spacing.lg)

                    row("rectangle.portrait.and.arrow.right", "Sign out", muted: true) {
                        signOut()
                    }
                }
                .padding(.vertical, 4)
            }

            Spacer(minLength: 0)

            Text("Citisen v1.0")
                .font(.caption12)
                .foregroundStyle(AppColor.textTertiary)
                .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
                .padding(.bottom, 32)
        }
        .frame(width: 300)
        .background(AppColor.surfaceElevated)
        .clipShape(RoundedCorners(tl: 16, bl: 16))
        .overlay(
            Rectangle()
                .fill(AppColor.divider)
                .frame(width: 0.5)
                .frame(maxHeight: .infinity),
            alignment: .leading
        )
    }

    private var header: some View {
        VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 8) {
            Avatar(initials: prefs.user.initials, size: 48)
            Text(prefs.user.displayName)
                .font(.headline17)
                .foregroundStyle(AppColor.textPrimary)
            Text(city.activeCity.displayName)
                .font(.footnote13)
                .foregroundStyle(AppColor.textSecondary)
        }
    }

    private func row(_ icon: String, _ label: String, muted: Bool = false, action: @escaping () -> Void) -> some View {

            HStack(spacing: 14) {
                Image(systemName: icon)

        router.popToRoot()

}

struct HamburgerMenuView: View {
    @Environment(AppRouter.self)
    private var router
    @Environment(UserPreferencesService.self)
    private var prefs
    @Environment(CityService.self)
    private var city
    @Environment(\.openURL)
    private var openURL

    @State private var showAbout = false
    @State private var showPrivacy = false
    @State private var showFeedbackAlert = false

    var body: some View {
        ZStack(alignment: .trailing) {
            if router.isHamburgerOpen {
                Color.black.opacity(0.4)
                    .ignoresSafeArea()
                    .contentShape(Rectangle())
                    .onTapGesture { router.closeHamburger() }
                    .transition(.opacity)

                drawer
                    .transition(.move(edge: .trailing))
            }
        }
        .animation(.spring(response: 0.35, dampingFraction: 0.85), value: router.isHamburgerOpen)
        .sheet(isPresented: $showAbout) {
            if let url = URL(string: AppConfig.aboutURLString) {
                SafariView(url: url)
            }
        }
        .sheet(isPresented: $showPrivacy) {
            if let url = URL(string: AppConfig.privacyURLString) {
                SafariView(url: url)
            }
        }
        .alert("Feedback", isPresented: $showFeedbackAlert) {
            Button("Send email") {
                if let url = URL(string: "mailto:\(AppConfig.feedbackEmail)?subject=Citisen%20feedback") {
                    openURL(url)
                }
            }
            Button("Cancel", role: .cancel) {}
        } message: {
            Text("Reach us at \(AppConfig.feedbackEmail).")
        }
    }

    private var drawer: some View {
        VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 0) {
            header
                .padding(.top, 64)
                .padding(.horizontal, Spacing.lg)
                .padding(.bottom, Spacing.lg)

            Divider().background(AppColor.dividerSoft).padding(.horizontal, Spacing.lg)

            ScrollView {
                VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 0) {
                    row("magnifyingglass", "Search") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.present(.search)
                    }
                    row("bookmark", "Saved") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.push(.saved)
                    }
                    row("map", "Collections") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.push(.saved)
                    }
                    row("mappin.circle", "Change city") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.present(.citySwitcher)
                    }

                    Divider().background(AppColor.dividerSoft).padding(.vertical, 8).padding(.horizontal, Spacing.lg)

                    row("gearshape", "Preferences") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.push(.profile)
                    }
                    row("person.crop.circle", "Profile") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        router.push(.profile)
                    }
                    row("info.circle", "About Citisen") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        showAbout = true
                    }
                    row("lock.shield", "Privacy") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        showPrivacy = true
                    }
                    row("envelope", "Send feedback") {
                        router.closeHamburger()
                        showFeedbackAlert = true
                    }

                    Divider().background(AppColor.dividerSoft).padding(.vertical, 8).padding(.horizontal, Spacing.lg)

                    row("rectangle.portrait.and.arrow.right", "Sign out", muted: true) {
                        signOut()
                    }
                }
                .padding(.vertical, 4)
            }

            Spacer(minLength: 0)

            Text("Citisen v1.0")
                .font(.caption12)
                .foregroundStyle(AppColor.textTertiary)
                .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
                .padding(.bottom, 32)
        }
        .frame(width: 300)
        .background(AppColor.surfaceElevated)
        .clipShape(RoundedCorners(tl: 16, bl: 16))
        .overlay(
            Rectangle()
                .fill(AppColor.divider)
                .frame(width: 0.5)
                .frame(maxHeight: .infinity),
            alignment: .leading
        )
    }

    private var header: some View {
        VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 8) {
            Avatar(initials: prefs.user.initials, size: 48)
            Text(prefs.user.displayName)
                .font(.headline17)
                .foregroundStyle(AppColor.textPrimary)
            Text(city.activeCity.displayName)
                .font(.footnote13)
                .foregroundStyle(AppColor.textSecondary)
        }
    }

    private func row(_ icon: String, _ label: String, muted: Bool = false, action: @escaping () -> Void) -> some View {
        Button(action: action) {
            HStack(spacing: 14) {
                Image(systemName: icon)
                    .font(.system(size: 20))
                    .foregroundStyle(AppColor.textSecondary)
                    .frame(width: 24)
                Text(label)
                    .font(.system(size: 16))

                Spacer(minLength: 0)
                if !muted {
                    Image(systemName: "chevron.right")
                        .font(.caption12)
                        .foregroundStyle(AppColor.textTertiary)
                }
            }
            .padding(.horizontal, Spacing.lg)
            .frame(minHeight: 52)
            .contentShape(Rectangle())
        }
        .buttonStyle(.plain)
    }

    private func signOut() {
        router.closeHamburger()
        @Bindable var prefs = prefs
        prefs.onboardingComplete = false
        router.popToRoot()
    }
}
